- `PATCH|DELETE /v1/sites/{site}/access/users/{user}`: update, disable, reset a
  password or delete a reader;
- `POST /v1/sites/{site}/access/users/{user}/revoke-sessions`: revoke every
  active session for one reader;
- `GET|POST /v1/sites/{site}/access/groups`: list or create groups;
- `PATCH|DELETE /v1/sites/{site}/access/groups/{group}`: rename or delete a
  group;
- `PUT /v1/sites/{site}/access/groups/{group}/members`: replace group members;
- `GET|POST /v1/sites/{site}/access/rules`: list or create exact/prefix rules;
- `PATCH|DELETE /v1/sites/{site}/access/rules/{rule}`: update or delete a draft
  policy; the response signals that a rebuild is required.

Reader routes run on each published site host:

- `GET|POST /_contentkit/login`: signed-CSRF login form and credential submit;
- `POST /_contentkit/logout`: revoke the current session and clear its cookie;
- `GET /_contentkit/session`: current public reader profile and group slugs;
- `GET /_contentkit/navigation.json`: protected navigation entries allowed to
  the current reader;
- `GET /_contentkit/search-index.json`: protected search entries allowed to the
  current reader.

Successful login sets an HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax reader cookie, Secure on HTTPS.
Sessions have a 12-hour idle expiry and seven-day absolute expiry; only an HMAC
of the random token is stored. Login attempts are limited to five per 15 minutes
per IP and normalized username. Anonymous HTML requests redirect to login,
protected media returns 401, a signed-in reader without a grant receives 403,
and every protected response uses `Cache-Control: private,no-store`.

Production requires an independent `CONTENTKIT_SESSION_SECRET`. See
`docs/ACCESS_CONTROL.md` for the end-user setup and troubleshooting guide.

### Verification and performance

`npm run test:contract` checks that the OpenAPI operations and report authoring
contract match the runtime. `npm run test:smoke` builds the real documentation,
wiki, knowledge-base, landing-page, changelog and report examples in one
release. `npm run test:e2e:local` compiles the binary, applies the real
PostgreSQL migration, uploads the real docs and report, publishes them, verifies
the report SVG and Markdown twin, verifies anonymous non-discovery, signs in as
a real reader and checks private search and delivery.

`npm run benchmark` builds 1,000 documents in two locales, including 200
protected documents. It separately times 50 reports with 200 charts and 24 rows
per chart, then measures 100,000 rule resolutions and an scrypt verification.
CI enforces the broad regression budgets in
`benchmarks/budgets.json` and uploads the JSON result. Reference results and
measurement caveats live in `docs/BENCHMARKS.md`.

---

## 21. Product analytics API

ContentKit derives privacy-safe, site-scoped product statistics from its own
PostgreSQL tables. It exposes them through the ordinary product API; it does
not know about a downstream cockpit, workflow engine, report schedule or
central storage bucket. Consumers reuse an existing site-scoped `content:read`
key rather than provisioning a reporting-specific credential.

The six read-only endpoints are:

- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/releases`: builds, outcomes, activation, output size
  and build duration;
- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/content`: items, revisions, publications and assets;
- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/readers`: privacy-safe authentication outcomes and
  sessions created;
- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/webhooks`: outbox events and delivery outcomes;
- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/audio`: jobs, outcomes, characters and duration;
- `/v1/sites/{site}/stats/engagement`: comments, contact submissions and
  anonymous feedback.

Every route accepts `bucket=hour|day|month|year`, RFC 3339 `from`/`to`, and
`tz=UTC`. It defaults to the previous 24 hours in hourly buckets, uses
inclusive-from/exclusive-to semantics, returns dense zero-filled buckets and
rejects invalid or excessive windows with 422. Hourly windows are capped at 31
days, daily at 366 days, monthly at five years and yearly at ten years.

Responses contain aggregates only: never Markdown, reader identities, keys,
webhook endpoints/payloads, storage paths, row IDs or error details. Reader
authentication facts contain only site, `success|failed|rate_limited`, and
timestamp and are retained for 400 days by default. External campaign,
acquisition, SEO and revenue data must be collected from the APIs that own
those facts and joined downstream. Full examples and collection guidance are
in `docs/PRODUCT_ANALYTICS.md`; the precise response contract is in
`docs/openapi.json`.
